series_title: Title IV-E Foster Care Maintenance (FCM) Program Operation Files
series_description: Reports and related correspondence submitted for review to establish Title IV-E reimbursement ceiling per diem amounts based on allowable costs (rate ceilings) for placing children in foster care. Files include JFS 02911 Single Cost Report, agreed-upon procedures reports, and related filing requirements utilized in the development of Title IV-E reimbursement ceiling per diem amounts. Also includes interim period cost reports and required backup documentation. Prior to SFY 2003, includes reports on costs of residential facilities (Residential Child Care Facility Report, JFS 02909), group homes, and purchased foster care (Purchased Family Foster Care, JFS 02910). Information is entered into SACWIS and rate information is posted on the internet. Information is also tracked in an Excel worksheet.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years

series_title: Title IV-E Juvenile Court Program (Administration Reimbursement)
series_description: Reports and related correspondence submitted to Bureau of Fiscal Accountability for review and reimbursement of administrative and training cost for children in custody of juvenile courts. These costs are reported on Juvenile Court Social Services Random Moment Allocation Quarterly Billing, JFS01797 form. Reports and related correspondence include Readiness Review documents.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office permanently.

series_description: Copies of county case records used for needs assessment review of abused, neglected or dependent children for purpose of determining compliance with Roe vs. Staples consent decree. This project will be repeated every 3 years.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until triennial project is completed ; Electronic: Retain until triennial project is completed

series_title: Community Based Family Resource & Support Program
series_description: Federal grant administered by the Children’s Trust Fund to provide community based family resource and support services to prevent child abuse and neglect. Files include copies of requests for applications, grant applications , grant agreements, scoring tools, award letters progress reports, invoices, HHS annual federal report, and related e-mail and correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 7 years following grant closure ; Paper: Retain in office 7 years following grant closure

series_title: Children's Trust Fund Report and Outreach
series_description: Report includes information on services provided and progress made toward achievement of performance goals. Outreach includes, but is not limited to: newsletters, biennial reports, legislative correspondence, as well as child abuse and neglect prevention month material and activities.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 7 years

series_title: Children's Trust Fund Regional Prevention Council
series_description: Regional Prevention Council Files include copies of requests for applications, grant applications, grant agreements, scoring tools, award letters, reports, invoices, documents pertaining to all eight (8) Regional Prevention Council activities, Ohio Children's Trust Fund (OCTF) Board Council Member Applications, County Board of Commissioners Regional Prevention Council appointment letters, and related email correspondences
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 7 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 7 years

series_title: Interstate Compact for the Placement of Children
series_description: Records pertaining to the placement of children within and outside the State of Ohio for adoption, foster care or residential treatment. Case files include: Interstate Compact Placement Request 100-A (JFS01661); Interstate Compact Placement Report on Child’s Placement Status 100-B(JFS 01662); Interstate Compact Transmittal (JFS 01640); Statement of Assurance(ODHS 1674); and Social and Medical HistoryBiological Mother and Father(ODHS 1616) BCII, FBI Central Registry, references, psychological, JFS 1653Medical statements and all other home study documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned, indexed (imaged)and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain microfilm or CD in office permanently.

series_description: Quarterly report of kinship data collected from county agencies for families in Ohio. Report includes the type of service and number of services being requested, kinship case information, and the number of families making the request.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office permanently.

series_title: Office of Families & Children(OFC) Automated Systems
series_description: Support and maintenance services for ODJFS and county Public Chi9ldren Services Agency (PCSA) data systems for the Office of Families and Children. Support includes system design, testing, modifications and technical assistance for Statewide Automated Child Welfare Information System Project.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Reports, correspondence, and/or memos relating to administration of the Child Abuse and Neglect Program.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain one year in office, four years Records Center. Review by State for possible trans- fer. Destroy if of no historical value to State Archives.

series_description: Case name of children and individual including: adoption decrees, social security numbers, birth certificates, tax forms information regarding youth in placement.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year. ; Electronic: Retain in office permanently

series_description: Record of potential fathers filed by mothers in adoption cases to search the system to see if the father is registered. Files may include the Registration form, JFS-01694 (Father’s form that is filed if he wants to prevent the adoption), letter of receipt, Search Request form, JFS-01695 (Mother’s form that is filed to look for a system match), and the Preliminary Certification Notice, JFS-01696, which contains names, birth dates, SS#’s, and addresses for the mother, father and child. Information is available on the Internet.
retention_statement: Electronic: Maintain data in Putative Father’s Registry system permanently. A copy will be retained off-site by MMIS for disaster recovery purposes. ; Paper: Retain in office 1 year after final certification is issued

series_description: This record series includes reports on agency activities required by
retention_statement: Electronic: Upon publication, fulfill the requirements of O.R.C. 149.11. Destroy excess paper copies when no longer of administrative value.

series_title: Family and Customer Support Center Help Desk
series_description: Established to track complaints and services provided by county and public children’s service agencies. Services include adoption, and cases of child abuse and neglect. Manual log contains case information, name, requester, policy clarification and response or action taken. Also legislative inquiries are tracked, documenting the number of calls or letters received and responses.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 60 days, image

series_description: Children's Trust Fund Board records regarding child abuse /neglect prevention policy and funding under OCTF. Files include board members appointment's documentation by laws and amendments meeting agendas, hand-outs, confidential financial disclosure information and minutes. OCTF strategic plan, fiscal reports and related correspondence. The board meets 4 times per year.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 2 years ; Paper: Retain in office 2 years

series_description: Monitoring reviews and related correspondence of services provided to families, e.g. child abuse assessment and investigation, foster care, adoption to ensure compliance with Administrative Code Rules, case plan guidelines and adoption requirements. Mandated by federal regulations for receiving Title IV-B and Title IV-E funding
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years

series_title: Special Education EMIS Data Collection Forms
series_description: Forms used to record special education data for submission to EMIS (Education Management Information System). Federal EDGAR rule 100b.734 specifies 5 years
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Title IV-E kinship subsidy program for children/youth/young adults age 0 to 21. Includes application, redetermination, approval, denial, disability, and termination forms. Includes eligibility documents such as legal custody, home study, educational, employment, and disability records. Includes kinship caregiver support related documents such as tax forms, mortgage statements, utility statements, cell phone statements, auto insurance policies, state and federal criminal background checks. Includes task completion checklists and case activity notes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain office permanently

series_description: These manuals are created for the Districts to follow policies and procedures for state testing: Directions for Administration; District Test Coordinator; Building Test Coordinator Manuals; Supplemental Instructions for Paper Testing; and Test Administration Manuals. These manuals are updated annually for every Fall, Spring and optional summer testing for each school year.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_description: Blank Test Books without student responses. There may be up to 10 forms of each paper test and special version forms.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for five (5) years after administration of test. Review by State before disposaL ; Electronic: Retain for five (5) years after administration of test. Review by State Archives before disposaL

series_title: District fiscal monitoring files (Management Assistant Reviews) "MARS"
series_description: District educational financial records regarding how a district spends its special education funding.
retention_statement: Paper: Records must be maintained until the next regular audit by the Auditor of State. Once the audit has been performed, the records can be destroyed. Records must be maintained until they are audited by the Auditor of State. Although these are regular auditors, the period between the audits can vary. ; Electronic: Records must be maintained until the next regular audit by the Auditor of State. Once the audit has been performed, the records can be destroyed. Records must be maintained until they are audited by the Auditor of State. Although these are regular auditors, the period between the audits can vary.

series_description: Log of legislative inquiries received from the Office of External Affairs which is used to track service actions and complaints. Log includes tracking number, date request was received, the requester, reason for the complaint, and the response date.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 2 years

series_title: Minority Families Reference and Resource File
series_description: Articles, brochures, books, pamphlets, speaker lists, and other reference materials pertaining to minority families and minority adoption.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in records center ; Electronic: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in records center

series_description: Includes Reports and Action Plans as a result of the monitoring review. Once review is closed, the file will contain all transient records; district self-reports, any district documents sent for review, and any previous review materials on file.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain reports and action plans for five (5) years ; Paper: Retain reports and action plans for five (5) years

series_description: Training Records and learning transcript records for trainings participants who are required to meet yearly training requirements outlined in the OAC, as well as participants earning trainings to acquire credentials (e.g.. adoption assessors, foster parents, domestic violence requirements). Electronic copies of all deliverable products created by vendors to support the Ohio Child Welfare Training Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for three years. ; Electronic: Retain in office for three years.

series_title: The Public Children Service Agencies (PCSA) Staff Exit Survey System
series_description: The Public Children Service Agencies (PCSA) Staff Exit Survey System is owned by Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS), Office of Families and Children (OFC), used for Public Children Service Agencies' (PCSA) employees who have separated from the PCSA. This electronic record consists of but not limited to: Employee's Name, PCSA, Dates of employment, Resigned or Terminated, Reason for separation, Employee's OHID, Recruitment and Retention Strategies, Supervisor's Name and work e-mail address
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 50 years

series_description: Public and private agency licensing records, including items necessary to determine agency compliance with licensing rules. Forms may include: JFS 01200,01268, 01270, 01271, 01276, 01282, 01283, 01290, 01324, 01327, 01330, 01339, 01340, 01342, 01344,01345, 01346, 01358,01359, 01376, 01377, 01390, 01391, 01392, 03326, 03327, 03328, 03329. Included also will be copies of child welfare agency written policies, approvals, financial audits, tables of organization, position descriptions, corrective action plans, articles of incorporation, BCI and FBI results and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 4 years in the records center

series_description: Putative Father Registry records consist of biological father inquiries, potential adoption documents, including: child’s name, social security number, race, biological mother's name. All information is captured on a JFS 01694.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: Rules, policies and background materials relating to the Placement Program. Also includes ORC references that generate the need for the rules and rule changes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office 3 years

series_title: Cash Food Policy Outreach Technical Assistance
series_description: The rules contained reflect chapters 1000 - 4000 of the Ohio Administrative Code. The documents also contain information regarding the clearance comments and process for the rules during the five-year rule review process. Including but not limited to Temporary Assistance Needy Family (TANF), United State Department Agriculture (USDA), Ohio Workforce (OWF), Kinship Caregiver which are services applied to a family dynamic.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 6 years ; Electronic: Retain 6 years

series_title: Child Care Licensing, Type B Home and Step Up To Quality Files
series_description: Records include receipted license application forms, licensees'' compliance statement and related documentation which serves as evidence of compliance with statutory requirements, reports of facility inspections by building, fire and health departments, inspection reports and related investigatory summaries of the centers. Files may also include copies of medical statements, material mandated documents of administrative hearings and Common Pleas Court proceedings, alleged operations, daycamps and additional license documentation. Step Up To Quality records may contain application, Star Rating Certificate, Onsite Inspections and visits, correction action and compliance, deferral and correspondence documents, appeals, supporting documents and additional license documentation. Type B Home License reports may contain electronic and paper Type B Home applications and county recommendations for licensure, annual inspection documents including but not limited to: home inspections, records reviews, and background check reviews, corrective action plans, complaints, appeals and revocation
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain in office 2 years after Open centers files: 1. 5 documents shall be kept for the life of the licensed program; A. Initial application material; B. Permits; and C. License Amendments E. Enforcements documents. 2. The other types of documents shall be purged 5 years after the scanned date. Closed center files: Retain for 2 years from date of center closure.

series_description: Files include adjustment forms from providers of publicly funded child care and accompanying email from county staff. These forms document child attendance from the provider for payment changes for authorized services.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 3 years from the date of center closure

series_description: Registry of cases from the counties that tracks incidents of child abuse and neglect. Information is entered into FACSIS, Sacwis Families and Children Services Information System. Registry includes principals involved in the cases, e.g. alleged perpetrators, victims and caretakers, SS#, driver’s license information, and protective service alerts.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years

series_title: Special Education case-load ratios and classroom size waivers
series_description: Educational agency requests and documentation to waive special education classroom and educator case load ratio work requirements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper in operating area until imaged and indexed. Once confirmed the paper copy has been checked for accuracy, quality, and completeness ; Electronic: Retain the electronic record for three (3) years;

series_title: Formal Written Complaints, Due Process Hearings and Appeals Case Files
series_description: The case files of due process hearing requests and state level appeals. 34 C.F.R. 300.507 (Filing a due process complaint) and 34 C.F.R. 300.514 (Finality of decision; appeal; impartial review). The case files for formal written complaints (state complaints) filed under 34 C.F.R. 300.152 (Minimum state complaint procedures)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain five years ; Electronic: Retain five years

series_title: Direct Services & Mediation Case Management Files – Special Education
series_description: Contain correspondence and other pertinent information relative to direct facilitation and direct mediation requested involving the provision of special education programs and services to suspected students with disabilities and students with suspected disabilities.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain three (3) years after case is closed ; Paper: Retain three (3) years after case is closed

series_description: Reviews and related correspondence documenting county agency child abuse and neglect cases, including reports of specific agency action to address instances of neglect and abuse. Most of the hard copy records are maintained in district offices. Copies of the correspondence are maintained on-site.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 60 days after imaging and ; Electronic: Retain in office 10 years.

series_description: Include project applications, fiscal reports, activity reports, on site monitoring reports, correspondence regarding projects and child count data for Title VI-B projects.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain three years, after grant closure. provided audit has been conducted by Auditor of State, the audit report is released, and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain three years, after grant closure. provided audit has been conducted by Auditor of State, the audit report is released, and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_title: Title IV-B Annual Progress and Services Reporting
series_description: File includes families that contain names and address to Title IV-B Funding Request Documents and Financial Reporting.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years. ; Electronic: Retain in the office 5 years.

series_description: Pregnancy-Associated Mortality Review (PAMR) records. Records of any services to women who died during pregnancy or within one year of pregnancy. These records can include: Healthcare Records: Admission history for all hospital admissions, Autopsy Report, Physical examination records from all hospital admissions, Coroner’s Notes, Declaration of Death, Discharge summary for all hospital admissions, Emergency Medical Services (EMS) Transport Records, Labor and Delivery Records, Prenatal Care Records, Postpartum Care Records, Social Service Records, Mental Health Records, Substance Use Disorder Records, Plan of Safe Care, Operative and Procedure Reports, Nursing Notes, Physician Progress Notes, Laboratory Test Results, Anesthesia Records, Consultation and Referral Records, Vitals Flow Sheets, Toxicology Report, Investigator’s Notes, Legal Records, Other: Complete Records Requested for care two years preceding death. Police Reports: 911-Detail Reports, MVA Crash Reports, Computer Aided Dispatch (CAD)-Reports, Motor Vehicle Accidents (MVA) Forensic Reports, Incident Reports, Arrest Reports, Incident Reports, Investigative Reports, Missing Person Reports and/or "be on the look out" (BOLO) Reports.
retention_statement: Paper: The PAMR Committee annually reviews pregnancy-associated deaths. The records they review will be disposed of 7 years after the completion of PAMR committee review. ; Electronic: The PAMR Committee annually reviews pregnancy-associated deaths. The records they review will be disposed of 7 years after the completion of PAMR committee review.

series_title: Children's Trust Fund- Prevent Child Abuse Ohio
series_description: Records relating to Children’s Trust Fund activities as the Prevent Child Abuse America Ohio Chapter. Includes capacity building plan and e-mail and correspondence related to Ohio Chapter services, communications, public policy, and marketing. Marketing includes, but is not limited to newsletters, reports and Prevent Child Abuse Ohio materials and activities.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 5 years
